step_by_step_thinking: Valine and isoleucine (options A and D) do not have ionizable groups that would create an electrically charged state under normal cellular conditions. Aspartic acid (option B), despite having acidic properties, does not contribute to overall net positive charge as it remains negatively charged due to its carboxyl group being protonated below pKa values typically found near neutral pH levels. This leaves arginine (option C). With multiple basic side chains including guanidino (-NH2) functional groups, this positively-charged residue can be present even when solution pH approximates neutrality because such bases remain deprotonated until reaching much higher local concentrations or more strongly alkaline environments than encountered inside cells where cytosolic pH tends towards around 7.4. **Answer Choice:** C: Arginine The presence of multiple basic nitrogen-containing side chains makes arginine capable of maintaining a positive charge at physiological pH levels compared to other listed residues which either lack or cannot sustain significant positive charges under those conditions. Therefore,
raw_response: Valine and isoleucine (options A and D) do not have ionizable groups that would create an electrically charged state under normal cellular conditions. Aspartic acid (option B), despite having acidic properties, does not contribute to overall net positive charge as it remains negatively charged due to its carboxyl group being protonated below pKa values typically found near neutral pH levels. This leaves arginine (option C). With multiple basic side chains including guanidino (-NH2) functional groups, this positively-charged residue can be present even when solution pH approximates neutrality because such bases remain deprotonated until reaching much higher local concentrations or more strongly alkaline environments than encountered inside cells where cytosolic pH tends towards around 7.4. **Answer Choice:** C: Arginine The presence of multiple basic nitrogen-containing side chains makes arginine capable of maintaining a positive charge at physiological pH levels compared to other listed residues which either lack or cannot sustain significant positive charges under those conditions. Therefore, option C, Arginine, correctly represents the amino acid with a positive charge at physiological pH.
